_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn thinking processes suited to different contexts and when and how to use them, including for problem-solving.

_source.w: We learn thinking processes suited to different contexts and when and how to use them, including for problem-solving.
y: Different situations call for different thinking approaches — knowing which to use is a crucial skill.
Being strategic about our thinking processes makes us more effective and efficient.
This metacognitive awareness is what distinguishes expert thinkers from novices.
eg: We select appropriate thinking processes — analytical, creative, reflective — for different tasks.
We use a systematic problem-solving approach: define, brainstorm, evaluate, implement, review.
We apply lateral thinking when conventional approaches are not working.
We discuss when to use deductive reasoning versus inductive reasoning.
We reflect on our thinking process after completing a task and identify what worked.
We adapt our thinking approach when our first strategy is not producing results.
